blocks.text: The LIN-connected A.C. generator converts a portion of the engine output into electric power via the drive belt, receives instructions from the PGM-FI ECU via LIN on the voltage to be generated, and generates power accordingly The only connection terminals between the A.C. generator and the vehicle side are the +B terminal (output terminal) and LIN communication terminal (1P connector ), and the enclosure of the A.C. generator is the GND. The power supply for regulator operation is connected internally using the +B terminal. the A.C. generator has a self-diagnostic function in the regulator and sends the self-diagnostic results to the PGM-FI ECU via LIN upon request from the PGM-FI ECU. If the A.C. generator internal temperature (regulator temperature) remains above a predetermined value for a predetermined period of time, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores a DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows: Ignition switch/ ENGINE START STOP switch
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: A.C. When the generator internal temperature (regulator temperature) remains above 190°C for more than 60 seconds.
If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory without the PGM-FI warning light being turned on.
Temporary DTCs, DTCs and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
